data.content:
The file is generated (confirmed by the log message), but it's not added to the `Compile` item group, so the C# compiler never sees it.

**Fix:** Add a `<Compile Include>` item after writing the file so MSBuild passes it to the compiler.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in the compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` and generates the file, but it doesn't add the generated file to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild needs to know about the file so the compiler includes it.

The fix is to add an `<ItemGroup>` with a `<Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />` inside the target, after the file is generated. This tells MSBuild to include it in the compilation.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in the compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

  </Target>", "new_str": "    <Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

  </Target>

data.content:
**Root cause:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote the `.cs` file to disk but never added it to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild only compiles files it knows about through that item group.

**Fix:** Added `<Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />` inside the target (after file generation) so the compiler picks it up.
